conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My phone battery has been draining faster than usual lately.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to renew my passport before it expires this winter.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Concept-seeded retrieval alone misses queries that name no detected concept -- we need a second retrieval path.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Could switch to embedding-only retrieval and drop the concept-graph path entirely.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched to a different brand of coffee and I'm not sure I like it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Embedding-only would throw away the ontology structure that already works well for concept-named queries -- combine both: keyword/concept-graph retrieval plus embedding retrieval feeding the same candidate pool, then let DeterministicRanker's blended score sort them out.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: It's supposed to rain most of next week according to the forecast.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: the two retrieval paths must never be allowed to double-count the same KnowledgeObject as two separate candidates with independent scores.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister's flight got delayed by a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked: both retrievers can independently surface the same KnowledgeObject, and naively merging the lists creates exactly that duplicate-candidate problem.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office plants finally got watered after being neglected for weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decided: dedupe by knowledge_object_id when merging the two candidate lists, keeping the higher of the two raw scores before ranking. Unblocks the merge.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Implemented HybridCandidateRetriever combining both paths with the dedupe-by-id merge.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Added a test with a query that has no concept match at all, confirming keyword overlap alone now surfaces the memory.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The dentist appointment I've been putting off is finally scheduled.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: should the two paths' raw scores be normalized onto the same scale before the dedupe keeps the 'higher' one, or is that comparison already meaningless across paths?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return that package before the return window closes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: benchmark hybrid retrieval against the concept-only baseline on the existing decisions/temporal categories to confirm no regression.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The wifi in the coffee shop I like to work from has been spotty.

ground_truth.current_objective: Add a second, embedding-based retrieval path alongside concept-graph retrieval, merged into one candidate pool.
active_decisions: Combine keyword/concept-graph retrieval and embedding retrieval into the same candidate pool, feeding DeterministicRanker's blended score.
Dedupe by knowledge_object_id when merging the two candidate lists, keeping the higher of the two raw scores.
superseded_or_rejected: Switching to embedding-only retrieval and dropping the concept-graph path entirely.
constraints: The two retrieval paths must never double-count the same KnowledgeObject as two separate candidates.
active_tasks: Benchmark hybrid retrieval against the concept-only baseline on decisions/temporal categories.
open_questions: Should the two paths' raw scores be normalized before the dedupe keeps the 'higher' one?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Benchmark hybrid retrieval against the concept-only baseline to confirm no regression on decisions/temporal categories.
resolved_blockers: Both retrievers surfacing the same KnowledgeObject and naively merging creating duplicates.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Combine keyword/concept-graph retrieval and embedding retrieval into the same candidate pool, feeding DeterministicRanker's blended score.
Dedupe by knowledge_object_id when merging the two candidate lists, keeping the higher of the two raw scores.
The two retrieval paths must never double-count the same KnowledgeObject as two separate candidates.
must_not_state: Switching to embedding-only retrieval and dropping the concept-graph path entirely, as a current or recommended approach
Both retrievers surfacing the same KnowledgeObject and naively merging creating duplicates, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending embedding-only retrieval as the path forward, dropping the concept-graph path.
must_prioritize: Benchmark hybrid retrieval against the concept-only baseline to confirm no regression on decisions/temporal categories.
